description: Length-matched V2 facts for antibiotic resistance domain.

facts.context: Resistance genes spread between bacteria primarily via:
facts.truth: horizontal gene transfer including plasmids and transduction
distractors: only vertical inheritance from parent to daughter cells
spontaneous mutations arising independently in each cell
direct environmental chemical exposure altering genomes

facts.truth: targeting and cutting specific bacterial essential genes
distractors: broadly stimulating host immune cells against infection
universally blocking all bacterial metabolic pathways
indiscriminately degrading DNA in all surrounding cells

facts.truth: high target specificity and co-evolutionary adaptability
distractors: broader spectrum activity than most chemical antibiotics
permanent elimination of all bacterial resistance genes
exemption from clinical regulatory approval processes

facts.context: The antibiotic discovery gap since the 1980s results from:
facts.truth: low return on investment and difficult novel target identification
distractors: all possible antibiotic chemical scaffolds already being explored
bacteria ceasing to develop any new forms of drug resistance
regulatory agencies systematically blocking new antibiotic research

facts.context: Efflux pump resistance mechanisms work by:
facts.truth: actively transporting antibiotics out of bacterial cells
distractors: blocking antibiotic entry through modified outer membranes
chemically converting antibiotics into biologically inactive forms
producing degradative enzymes that hydrolyze the antibiotic

facts.context: Biofilm infections are harder to treat because:
facts.truth: extracellular matrix shields bacteria and limits drug penetration
distractors: biofilm bacteria transform into genetically distinct new species
planktonic cells respond less effectively to antibiotic treatment
biofilms exclusively colonize synthetic non-biological surface types

facts.context: Narrow-spectrum antibiotics are preferred because they:
facts.truth: preserve beneficial microbiome and reduce resistance selection pressure
distractors: cost significantly less to manufacture than broad-spectrum alternatives
achieve faster bactericidal killing than broad-spectrum counterparts
treat all known infection types without requiring susceptibility testing

facts.truth: optimize prescribing to slow resistance while preserving clinical efficacy
distractors: eliminate all antibiotic prescriptions from clinical practice entirely
primarily reduce hospital expenditure without addressing resistance rates
increase total antibiotic consumption to achieve improved patient outcomes

facts.context: Persister cells survive antibiotic treatment by:
facts.truth: entering a dormant metabolic state without genetic resistance
distractors: acquiring point mutations that enzymatically inactivate antibiotics
expressing high-capacity efflux pumps that export all drug molecules
forming protective extracellular capsules that exclude antibiotic entry

facts.truth: reducing resistance emergence through simultaneous multiple targeting
distractors: consistently producing greater toxicity than single-agent monotherapy
providing no measurable clinical advantage over individual drug regimens
being indicated exclusively when confirmed resistance already is present

facts.context: Agricultural antibiotic use contributes to resistance by:
facts.truth: selecting resistant bacteria that subsequently transfer to humans
distractors: having no documented impact on clinically relevant human resistance
exclusively affecting resistance among crop and plant pathogen species
being comprehensively banned worldwide thus preventing all such issues

facts.context: Antimicrobial peptides as alternatives face challenges including:
facts.truth: poor serum stability and dose-limiting host cell toxicity
distractors: complete absence of measurable antibacterial activity in vitro
fundamental inability of bacteria to evolve peptide resistance
manufacturing costs so low they undermine commercial feasibility
